tier2.quote: Houston Methodist received a T32 training grant to equip the next generation of clinicians and scientists to address antimicrobial resistance and tackle its dire impact on human health.
tier2.claimText: Houston Methodist holds a T32 training grant for clinician-scientist training in antimicrobial resistance.
tier2.whyA1Missed: A1 did not flag the T32 grant as a trainee-pathway signal.
tier2.tierAssignmentRationale: T32 funds postdoctoral/clinician-scientist training — Tier 2 trainee content.

tier2.claimText: Houston Methodist Academic Institute advertises 67,000 learners (system-wide aggregate).
tier2.limitations: Aggregate across all learner categories; not USCE-specific.
tier2.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the 71 GME programs stat but missed the adjacent learner volume figure.

tier3.claimText: Houston Methodist reports 883 faculty with academic appointments — signals attending/faculty employment scale.
tier3.limitations: Aggregate faculty count, not a specific job posting.
tier3.whyA1Missed: A1 emitted only the GME-program stat; missed the adjacent faculty-appointment count signaling career-track scale.
tier3.tierAssignmentRationale: Faculty appointment count is a post-trainee/attending career signal — Tier 3.

a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: Quote 'Medical Student Rotations & Observerships' is a navigation/section header on a system-level Academic Institute hub page — not a substantive statement of offering. Visibility should not be PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE without subpage confirmation.

Page is Houston Methodist Academic Institute (system/medical-school level spanning campuses incl. The Woodlands). Statement about 'medical student rotations at Houston Methodist' is not campus-specific to Houston Methodist Hospital.

Text describes inpatient/subspecialist settings 'in a range of fields:' but the list that follows is just a link label ('Medical Student Rotations & Observerships'). No fields enumerated; eligibility (own vs visiting) unstated.
A1 already flagged the scope ambiguity; A2 confirms the Academic Institute hub is medical-school/system level (mentions The Woodlands expansion), not Houston Methodist Hospital campus-specific.

scopeConflictsDetectedInA2.explanation: Page covers Academic Institute spanning multiple campuses including 'Houston Methodist The Woodlands' expansion. Cannot attribute rotations statement specifically to Houston Methodist Hospital campus.
Same Academic Institute hub scope — rotation structure description is system/medical-school level, not campus-specific.

additionalUnresolveds: Page mentions 'Houston Methodist The Woodlands' campus expansion but does not state whether medical student rotations or observerships are offered at that campus specifically.
'Range of fields' is promised with a colon but the field list is not present on this hub page — resolution requires the linked Rotations & Observerships subpage.
ENMED 'Physicianeer' program eligibility (US MD applicants only? open to IMGs?) is not stated.
recommendedA3Focus: Confirm no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim is published from this Academic Institute hub URL — scope is medical-school/system level.
Verify Houston Methodist Hospital campus-specific applicability is established only via a subpage citing the hospital by name.
Downgrade or gate hm-medu-01/02/03 pending subpage with explicit eligibility and contact details.
Confirm undergraduate/high-school summer research internship (hm-medu-a2-t1-03) is not surfaced as medical-student USCE.

newClaims.quote: Our rotations and observerships provide in-depth, hands-on experience in a range of fields.
newClaims.claimText: Houston Methodist describes rotations and observerships as offering in-depth, hands-on experience across a range of fields.
newClaims.limitations: Conflates rotations with observerships as hands-on; conflicts with typical non-hands-on observership norm.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Tier 1 USCE quality attribute on a medical-student rotations page.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured existence of rotations/observerships but missed the 'hands-on, in-depth' descriptor that defines clinical character.

newClaims.quote: Residents learn in a variety of settings, such as working with inpatient teams and one-on-one with subspecialists.
newClaims.claimText: Learning settings described as inpatient teams and one-on-one work with subspecialists.
newClaims.limitations: Sentence uses 'Residents' rather than 'medical students,' producing audience ambiguity on a Medical Student Rotations page.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Tier 1 structural detail about rotation/observership setting.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 did not extract the learning-settings sentence.

newClaims.claimText: Global Education Office contact details (email/phone/URL) not stated on this page despite being the directed pathway for international observers.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Critical Tier 1 IMG-pathway field absent.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 listed it as an unresolved note but did not emit a structured MISSING_FIELD claim.

newClaims.claimText: Specific specialties/departments accepting medical student rotations or observerships are not enumerated on this page.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Specialty enumeration is a Tier 1 USCE attribute and is absent.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: Page only says 'range of fields' without naming them; A1 didn't flag this.

a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: Supporting quote says rotations/observerships 'give residents the proper amount of time and responsibility' — wording targets residents rather than medical students, despite the page being titled Medical Student Rotations. Audience inference for USCE eligibility is unclear.
Audience pathway is for 'observe a clinical practice' (observership scope), not visiting medical student electives. Lane INTERNATIONAL_MEDICAL_STUDENT likely overstates; IMG_OBSERVERSHIP fits better.

Same quote: pathway directs international physicians/residents/students to observe via Global Education Office. Action is observation, not a visiting student elective; lane should be IMG_OBSERVERSHIP.

additionalUnresolveds: Page conflates 'rotations' and 'observerships' as both hands-on; whether the observership pathway is truly non-hands-on (per ECFMG norms) or whether 'rotation' here implies a hands-on elective for medical students is not disambiguated.
Whether Houston Methodist's medical student rotations are open to visiting students from other US schools or restricted to home/affiliated students is not stated.
Campus applicability — Houston Methodist operates multiple hospitals; the page does not specify which campus(es) host rotations/observerships.
recommendedA3Focus: Confirm c1 is not promoted to PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE; quote uses 'residents' and does not state eligibility for outside medical students.
Verify c4/c5 international-pathway claims are filed under IMG_OBSERVERSHIP rather than INTERNATIONAL_MEDICAL_STUDENT given the verb 'observe.'
Check that no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim cites the system-level houstonmethodist.org URL without campus applicability proof.
Confirm 71 GME Programs (f1) remains FUTURE_LANE_ONLY and is not promoted to a USCE lane.
